text: AI is supercharging cyberattacks, with breaches now completing in as little as seventy-two minutes, CNN reports.
The Hacker News reveals attackers are using AI to automate phishing and lateral movement at machine speed.
CyberPress documented the Conduent breach, where eight terabytes of data were stolen in a single ransomware attack.
SharkStriker logged over forty major incidents in February alone, with healthcare and finance hit hardest.
Experts say traditional firewalls are no match for machine-speed threats.
As attackers leverage AI for unprecedented speed, defenders must adopt AI-driven security or risk catastrophic breaches.
This algorithmic arms race will make traditional cybersecurity tools obsolete within years.
